decisions.evidence: Court-filed Exhibit A from RECAP/CourtListener with docket URL, raw hash, CFTC complaint URL, and CFTC press URL.
decisions.reason: The artifact is a public court-record copy and strong source evidence, but the active V62 contract still requires owner/user-approved export rows or explicit approval before canonical intake mutation.

decisions.evidence: Exhibit A labels 5182 rows as SPOOF and the isolated verifier accepts their schema.
decisions.reason: SPOOF rows can remain high-quality direct positive candidates, subject to provenance approval.

decisions.evidence: The 1553 rows used as controls are Exhibit A rows labeled FLIP from the same Oystacher/3Red flip sequences.
decisions.reason: FLIP rows are same-defendant same-exhibit sequence rows, not source-owned normal/non-manipulation labels or report-negative matched controls under the current Board A contract.

decisions.evidence: Materialized split metrics pass across family, venue, symbol, and year when FLIP rows are treated as negatives.
decisions.reason: The split pass depends on treating FLIP rows as matched negatives, so it is a proxy signal until the control policy is approved.

decisions.evidence: No shared lock merge or live canonical intake mutation has occurred.
decisions.reason: Canonical mutation requires provenance approval plus valid matched controls or explicit user approval of the FLIP-control contract.

decisions.evidence: No accepted canonical row or validation-contract change exists after policy review.
decisions.reason: Provider/Auto-Quant/pre-Bayes/BBN/CatBoost/execution-tree rerun would be a proxy signal until canonical acceptance changes.

next_action: If the user approves RECAP/PACER provenance and explicitly approves FLIP rows as matched controls, merge through the shared lock and rerun the direct verifier, split calibration, provider, Auto-Quant, pre-Bayes/BBN, CatBoost/path-ranking, and execution-tree chain; otherwise source independent owner-approved normal controls.
